story.iWant: i18n ve logger kullanımı tüm servis ve controller'larda tam olarak entegre edilmiş olsun
story.soThat: multi-language support tam olsun ve console.log kullanılmasın, profesyonel logging standardı uygulanmış olsun
story.tasks: - Task 1: Audit Codebase for console.* Usage - Task 2: Audit Codebase for Hardcoded Strings - Task 3: Design Translation Key Structure - Task 4: Create Translation Files - Task 5: Verify i18n Module Configuration - Task 6-10: Refactor Services (Auth, Token, Users, Permissions, Files) - Task 11-15: Refactor Controllers (Auth, Users, Profile, Permissions, Files) - Task 16: Refactor Guards for Logger - Task 17: Refactor Interceptors for Logger - Task 18: Refactor Exception Filters - Task 19: Apply Logging Standards - Task 20: Test i18n Integration - Task 21: Verify No Console Usage - Task 22: Build Validation and Final Testing - Task 23: Update Unit Tests

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: - AC-8.5-3.1: Tüm Services i18n kullanıyor (AuthService, UsersService, PermissionsService, FilesService, TokenService) - AC-8.5-3.2: Tüm Controllers i18n kullanıyor (AuthController, UsersController, ProfileController, PermissionsController, FilesController) - AC-8.5-3.3: i18n translation dosyaları güncellendi (tr/en: errors.json, success.json, validation.json) - AC-8.5-3.4: Console.log'lar Logger service'e dönüştürüldü (main.ts bootstrap hariç) - AC-8.5-3.5: Logger Service tüm dosyalarda kullanılıyor (Services, Controllers, Guards, Interceptors, Filters) - AC-8.5-3.6: Logger context eklendi (her service/controller doğru context ismi kullanıyor) - AC-8.5-3.7: Translation key organization standardı uygulandı (errors.*, success.*, validation.*) - AC-8.5-3.8: AcceptLanguage header'dan dil algılanıyor (tr/en fallback) - AC-8.5-3.9: Logging standardı uygulandı (info, debug, error, warn levels) - AC-8.5-3.10: Build ve testler başarılı

docs.doc: Acceptance Criteria for i18n and logging integration: Services use I18nService for error/success messages, Controllers use i18n, translation files (tr/en: errors.json, success.json, validation.json), console.log replaced with Logger, Logger context in all components, AcceptLanguage header detection, logging standards (info, debug, error, warn levels)

i18n setup: nestjs-i18n module, Accept-Language header detection, default EN fallback. Logger setup: Winston structured logging (JSON format), log levels (debug, info, warn, error), console/file transports, sensitive data exclusion

Detailed I18nService interface (t() method, variable replacement), Winston Logger interface (debug, info, warn, error methods), log entry structure (timestamp, level, message, context), translation file structure (common.json, auth.json, users.json), environment variables (LOG_LEVEL, SENTRY_DSN)

Service error handling pattern with i18n: throw new NotFoundException(this.i18n.t('module.NOT_FOUND')). Logger pattern: private readonly logger = new Logger(ServiceName.name). Import organization: Libraries, DTOs, Services, Repositories, Entities, Interfaces, Enums

TransformResponseInterceptor already has i18n integration (success.json files created). No duplication needed. Services return raw data, interceptor wraps automatically. i18n success message keys: success.OPERATION_SUCCESSFUL, success.CREATED, success.UPDATED, success.DELETED

artifact.reason: Reference for how i18n service is tested and used
Winston logger implementation to be used instead of console.log
Needs I18nService injection and Logger integration (AC-8.5-3.1)
Needs I18nService injection and Logger integration (AC-8.5-3.1)
Needs I18nService injection and Logger integration (AC-8.5-3.1)
Needs I18nService injection and Logger integration (AC-8.5-3.1)
Has console.log usage, needs i18n and Logger integration (AC-8.5-3.2, 8.5-3.4)
Already has i18n integration (Story 8.5-2), reference for i18n usage pattern

constraints.constraint: I18nService MUST be injected in constructor: private readonly i18n: I18nService
Logger MUST be initialized as instance variable: private readonly logger = new Logger(ContextName.name)
Translation keys MUST follow format: category.SPECIFIC_KEY (e.g., errors.USER_NOT_FOUND, success.CREATED)
Error messages MUST use i18n: throw new NotFoundException(this.i18n.t('errors.USER_NOT_FOUND'))
NO console.log, console.error, console.warn, console.debug usage (except main.ts bootstrap)
NO hardcoded error/success messages - MUST use i18n keys
Import organization: Libraries → DTOs → Services → Repositories → Entities → Interfaces → Enums
Logger context naming: Use class name (e.g., AuthService.name, UsersController.name)
Log levels: log() for info, debug() for debugging, error() for exceptions, warn() for warnings
All service/controller tests MUST mock I18nService: { t: jest.fn((key) => key) }
All service/controller tests MUST mock Logger: { log: jest.fn(), error: jest.fn(), warn: jest.fn(), debug: jest.fn() }
Build MUST succeed: npm run build && npm run type-check

interfaces.interface: Multi-language translation service. Usage: this.i18n.t('errors.USER_NOT_FOUND') or this.i18n.t('auth.OTP_SENT', { args: { phone: '+905551234567' } })

NestJS Logger class. Methods: log(message: string), debug(message: string), error(message: string, trace?: string), warn(message: string). Usage: private readonly logger = new Logger(ServiceName.name); this.logger.log('User created successfully')

errors.json: Error message keys (USER_NOT_FOUND, INVALID_CREDENTIALS, PERMISSION_DENIED, etc.) success.json: Success message keys (OPERATION_SUCCESSFUL, CREATED, UPDATED, DELETED, UPLOADED, etc.) validation.json: Validation message keys (EMAIL_INVALID, PASSWORD_TOO_SHORT, REQUIRED_FIELD, etc.)

interface.name: Service i18n Integration
interface.signature: constructor(private readonly i18n: I18nService, private readonly logger: Logger = new Logger(ServiceName.name))

interfaces.interface: Services inject I18nService and initialize Logger. Use i18n.t() for all error/success messages. Use logger methods instead of console.log. Example: throw new BadRequestException(this.i18n.t('errors.USER_NOT_FOUND'))

interface.name: Controller i18n Integration
interface.signature: constructor(private readonly service: Service, private readonly i18n: I18nService)

interfaces.interface: Controllers inject I18nService and Logger. Response messages use i18n.t(). Logger logs request entry/exit. Example: this.logger.log(`Login request received for: ${email}`)

Exception filters inject I18nService and Logger. Translate error messages before sending to client. Log errors with context and stack trace. Example: this.logger.error('Exception caught', exception.stack)

tests.standards: Unit Testing Framework: Jest (already configured) Coverage Target: 90% for refactored services and controllers Mock Strategy: Mock I18nService with { t: jest.fn((key) => key) }, Mock Logger with { log: jest.fn(), error: jest.fn(), warn: jest.fn(), debug: jest.fn() } Test Pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert, clear test names describing behavior Integration Testing: Test i18n with different Accept-Language headers (tr/en) E2E Testing: Verify translation keys work end-to-end, verify logger output in console Build Validation: npm run build, npm run type-check, grep verification for console.* usage
tests.ideas: - AC-8.5-3.1: Test AuthService.login() throws NotFoundException with i18n key 'errors.USER_NOT_FOUND' - AC-8.5-3.1: Test UsersService.create() calls logger.log() with appropriate message - AC-8.5-3.2: Test UsersController.findAll() returns response with i18n translated message - AC-8.5-3.3: Test translation files exist (tr/en) and have required keys (errors.*, success.*, validation.*) - AC-8.5-3.4: Run grep for console.log in src/ (exclude main.ts), verify no matches - AC-8.5-3.5: Test SentryExceptionFilter has Logger injected and logs errors - AC-8.5-3.6: Test Logger context naming: verify AuthService logger context is 'AuthService' - AC-8.5-3.7: Test translation key organization: verify errors.USER_NOT_FOUND exists in errors.json - AC-8.5-3.8: Integration test: POST /auth/login with Accept-Language: tr returns Turkish error - AC-8.5-3.8: Integration test: POST /auth/login with Accept-Language: en returns English error - AC-8.5-3.9: Test logger.error() includes stack trace for exceptions - AC-8.5-3.10: Run npm run build, verify success - AC-8.5-3.10: Run npm run type-check, verify no TypeScript errors
